blurb: Working on the backend of an ecommerce system — order flows, integrations, and the unglamorous correctness work that keeps shops running.
Backend work on Qvickly's payment products — the corner of fintech where reconciliation is its own discipline and the numbers have to be right.
Eight years building and maintaining Nordisk E-handel, eValent Group's ecommerce platform. Long enough to learn every corner of the stack — and where the bodies were buried.
Generalist web work — small-business sites, custom CMS features, and whatever else the studio needed that week. The kind of role where the day's task list was whatever walked through the door.
Behind the camera at Bildstudio 8 — and across the chain that follows the shutter, from print to frame.
Twelve years leading development of UniNet's CMS — roadmap, releases, and keeping the core in shape as the product grew around it.
My one-person shop, kept open for small pilot projects and the experiments that didn't fit anywhere else.

Part-time educator and developer through StudentKraft, placed as a consultant at third-party companies. The memorable stretch: lead instructor on Lexicon's XML courses, back when XML was new enough to need teaching.
A small company where everyone wore every hat — technical support, phone sales, logistics, and deliveries, all on the network-equipment side.
Selling PCs and PC software to resellers across southern Sweden — roughly Halmstad and Växjö, and everything below.
Reseller sales for southern Sweden, doubling as the technical lead on IBM's networking and software lines.
Technical support on networking gear, plus product-management duties for IBM Software, AXIS print servers, and Citrix remote access — the early-internet office stack.
Front-line phone support and sales to computer resellers across Sweden — and time in the warehouse when the boxes needed shifting.
Behind the counter selling home computers, with the Atari range as my product-management beat. Equal parts retail and enthusiasm.
Second Lieutenant (Fänrik). Training conscripts in the field — mostly on the not-very-forgiving subject of directing artillery fire.
Mandatory military service — the year before the officer commission.
